summary: Write hook that creates a new service location (sede) for a vendor in vendor_locations/{vendorID}/locations/{locationID}. Follows the same direct-Firestore pattern as useCreateCarrierVehicle. The double-nested location schema (location.location.lat/lng) is required for compatibility with the onRequestCreated distribution trigger.

businessIntent.problem: Carrier admins need to register service locations so the distribution trigger (onRequestCreated) can match incoming freight requests to carriers operating in that geographic zone. Without at least one location, the vendor receives no requests.
businessIntent.expectedOutcome: Calling createLocation writes a document to vendor_locations/{vendorID}/locations/ with the correct nested schema and returns the created location object including the generated locationID.

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: hook-service-pattern
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: Write hook — async function, input validation, try/catch, returns created object. Same pattern as useCreateCarrierVehicle.

existingPatternsToFollow: Direct Firestore write — no httpsCallable, same pattern as useCreateCarrierVehicle
Generate doc ref with doc(collection(db, 'vendor_locations', vendorID, 'locations'))
Trim and validate all string inputs before writing
Return the created object with the generated locationID
ESM imports: { doc, setDoc, serverTimestamp, collection } from firebase/firestore
knownAntiPatternsToAvoid: Do not use httpsCallable — this is a direct client-side Firestore write
CRITICAL: location field must be double-nested: location: { location: { lat, lng } } — onRequestCreated reads locData.location.location.lat/lng
Do not flatten to location: { lat, lng } — the trigger will silently skip this vendor
Do not skip maxDistanceService — the trigger uses it for haversine distance check
Collection is vendor_locations (plural with S) — never vendor_location

architectureImpact.notes: The double-nested location schema is operationally critical — incorrect nesting silently breaks request distribution.

create.description: Write hook that creates vendor_locations/{vendorID}/locations/{locationID} with double-nested location schema. Returns { createLocation } async function.
Jest tests: throws when vendorID missing, throws when lat/lng missing, throws when maxDistanceService missing, successful write uses double-nested schema, returns locationID.

dataModelsTouched.notes: Path: vendor_locations/{vendorID}/locations/{locationID}. Required schema: { locationID, vendorID, name, address, location: { location: { lat, lng } }, maxDistanceService, status: 'active', createdAt, updatedAt }. Double-nested location is critical for onRequestCreated compatibility.

expectedPassingSignals: Written document has location.location.lat and location.location.lng (verify in setDoc mock args)
failureEscalationRules: If setDoc mock fails: reload testing-guide building block and verify mock import paths
If location schema is flat instead of double-nested: reload the plan knownAntiPatternsToAvoid and regenerate

steps: Generate useCreateLocation.ts following hook-service-pattern + useCreateCarrierVehicle reference
CRITICAL: verify generated code uses location: { location: { lat, lng } } double-nested shape
Generate useCreateLocation.test.ts with 5 test cases including schema validation assertion
Produce handoff package for Claude Code integration

claudeCodeIntegrationPlan.reason: Factory output is an integration candidate only — Claude Code must verify the double-nested location schema before committing.
steps: Verify useCreateLocation.ts writes location: { location: { lat, lng } } — grep or read the file
finalCommitChecklist: location field is double-nested: location: { location: { lat, lng } }

acceptanceCriteria: createLocation() throws if vendorID is missing
createLocation() throws if lat or lng is missing
createLocation() throws if maxDistanceService is missing
Written document has location.location.lat and location.location.lng (double-nested — matches onRequestCreated)
Written document has maxDistanceService as a number
